channel.title: AP News 全文 RSS（简体中文版）
channel.description: AP News 首页抓取 + 英译简

item.title: 以色列正在加沙境内修建长达数英里的土墙，以巩固其分裂
item.description:
CAIRO (AP) — The Israeli military is quietly but quickly building a giant earthen barrier separating the over 50% ofthe Gaza Stripit controls from the rest of the territory, according to satellite imagery, further entrenching the division of the tiny Palestinian enclave.
More than 23 kilometers (14 miles) have been constructed in recent months, running through Palestinian communities demolished by the military. That is equivalent to more than half the length ofthe coastal strip, which is some 40 kilometers (25 miles) long and 11 kilometers (7 miles) wide, and home to over 2 million Palestinians.
When presented with the satellite images, Israel’s military confirmed to The Associated Press it has built a physical barrier in the area of the so-called yellow line, the boundary to which Israeli troops withdrew under theOctober ceasefire dealwith Hamas. The line was envisioned in the U.S.-backed agreement as a temporary division of the territory pending a fuller Israeli withdrawal.
But as the truce stagnates, it appears Israel is digging in, heightening Palestinian fears that the line is morphing into a border. Neither U.S. Central Command, which is tasked with monitoring the ceasefire, nor theBoard of Peace, the body led by U.S. PresidentDonald Trumpmeant to eventually oversee Gaza, would comment on the barrier.
Along with the barrier, the military told AP it has developed a “security zone” around the line, equipped with intelligence and technological assets. It declined to give details on the route of the barrier when asked how far it would be extended. It said the barrier aimed to prevent infiltration and protect Israeli troops and Israeli communities near Gaza.
Since the ceasefire deal took hold, Palestinians in Gaza have feared Israel would not return the area of Gaza it controls. But few appear to be aware of the berms now being built around them — because of the danger of even approaching that area.
A portion of the berm network in southern Gaza was extended by more than 2 kilometers (1.2 miles) in two weeks this month, according to the satellite images provided by Planet Labs PBC.
The berm was about 500 meters (0.3 miles) long on July 1. By July 15, it was about 2.4 kilometers (1.5 miles), splicing the Israel-controlledruins of the city of Rafahfrom Muwasi, site of some of thelargest Palestinian tent camps.
If construction continues in its current direction, it will join up with the longest stretch of the barrier, which runs nearly unbroken for around 17 kilometers (10 miles) from the southern city of Khan Younis to near Gaza City, in the north.
Construction began on that stretch in February, and it appears work to extend it further south is continuing, according to the satellite images. More than a kilometer (0.6 miles) was added between June 21 and July 7, running close to an Israeli military base built on the ruins of the town of Bani Suheila, near Khan Younis.
In the far north of Gaza, several unconnected stretches of berms run just outside the largely leveled town of Beit Lahia.
The height of the berms in each place remains unclear. In some places, the barriers appear to run outside the yellow line.
Gaza’s population has been crowded into the coastal area west of the yellow line, most living in squalid tent camps and dependent on aid.
On the other side of the line, satellite imagery shows that Israeli forces have leveled the vast majority of buildings using bulldozers and explosives, virtually erasing several towns and Rafah, once home to more than a quarter million people.
They have carved new road networks and erected new bases on the ruins of Palestinian towns. Agricultural land has also been destroyed. The Israeli military says it is demolishing infrastructure used by Hamas.
The yellow line was never precisely defined, whether in the ceasefire deal or by Israeli or U.S. officials, and it has been inconsistently marked on the ground. The military told AP it is working to clearly mark the route of the yellow line to “reduce friction and prevent harm to uninvolved individuals.”
Israeli forces have killedPalestinians who came close to the lineor crossed it. The Israeli military says it fires on those believed to be militants threatening its troops. But those killed have included children and other civilians seemingly unaware of the line. Some soldiers have said they don’t always know who they are firing on and have ordersto kill anyone crossing the boundary.
Israeli forces have killed more than 1,100 Palestinians since the ceasefire began, most in airstrikes and drone strikes around Gaza. Militant attacks have killed five Israeli soldiers in the same period.
Early on Tuesday, an Israeli strike in Gaza City killed Firas al-Masri, his wife and their four children, ages 6 to 13, according to Shifa Hospital. Israel’s military only said that they conducted a strike on Hamas militants in the area at that time.
Firas’ brother, Ahmad al-Masri, lives nearby and said he heard the children’s voices at the moment of the blast.
“They were calling out to me, ‘Uncle! Uncle!’” he said. “But the fire was raging, and we couldn’t do anything.”
Under the ceasefire deal, Israel is eventually supposed to withdraw its troops further to the very edges of Gaza, to be replaced by an international security force. But implementation of the deal has been stalled for months.
A former U.N. diplomat now in charge of coordinating the ceasefire has blamed Hamas’failure to disarm. Sporadic talks to reach a formula for decommissioning its weapons appear to have made little progress, and Hamas accuses Israel in turn of violating the ceasefire.
The war began after Hamas-led militants attacked Israeli communities on Oct. 7, 2023, killing around 1,200 people, mostly civilians, and taking 251 others hostage. Israel responded with a punishing military offensive that has killed more than 73,000 people, according to the Gaza Health Ministry.
The ministry, which was part of therecently dissolvedHamas-run government, maintains detailed records viewed as generally reliable by U.N. agencies and international organizations. It does not distinguish between civilians and militants but says women and children make up around half of all fatalities.

item.title: 美军完成对伊朗的第11晚袭击，袭击掩盖了外交
item.description:
DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — The U.S. military said it completed the 11th night of strikes on Iran early Wednesday as attacks across the Middle East overshadowed diplomatic efforts in Pakistan to salvage aninterim ceasefire deal that has collapsed.
U.S. Central Command said targets included aircraft hangars and drone storage sites. The newest barrage came after President Donald Trump telegraphed that strikes would be intensifying.
Before the latest strikes, Iranian Interior Minister Eskandar Momeni visited Pakistan, a key mediator in the conflict, seeking to revamp diplomacy. But it was unclear what new arrangement might be reached to endthe war, which lately has become a battle for control over the Strait of Hormuz, a waterway vital to world energy supplies.
Speaking from the Oval Office on Tuesday, Trump gave a dim view on possible talks, saying the U.S. had “no interest in meeting.” He signaled that U.S. forces could soon target an area of Iran close to one of its main sites forenriching uranium.
With negotiations and shipping through theStrait of Hormuzlargely stalled, both sides have sought to gain leverage by targeting civilian infrastructure in the region relied on by millions of people.
A barrage of U.S. strikes hasn’t loosened Iran’s grip onthe strait, through which about one-fifth of the world’s crude oil and natural gas was shipped before the war.
The U.S. continued its airstrikes into Wednesday, with Iranian air defenses activating near the capital, Tehran. Iran’s state-run IRNA news agency reported that explosions rocked Bushehr, East Azerbaijan, Hamadan, Hormozgan, Khuzestan and Sistan and Baluchistan provinces.
That came after Iran attacked a tanker in the strait Tuesday, forcing the crew to abandon the ship. Iran also kept up attacks against U.S. allies in the region. Iran also launched attacks against Bahrain, Jordan and Kuwait.
The escalation pushed energy prices higher. The international benchmark for crude oil traded Wednesday above $92 a barrel and regular gasoline in the U.S. climbed to an average of $4 a gallon.
With U.S. midterm elections approaching in the fall, polls havepointed to the unpopularity of the waramong Americans, who have felt the pain of pricier gas and seennew casualtiesin a conflict that weeks ago seemed to be winding down.
The number of wounded U.S. personnel in the Iran war has grown to well over 500 troops, a U.S. official told The Associated Press. The total is higher than the Pentagon’s official tally on the Defense Casualty Analysis System, which currently shows 482 wounded.
The U.S. official, who was not authorized to discuss the matter with the media and spoke on condition of anonymity, said the system has a significant lag in reporting injuries. The official said that was a function of its design and not intentional.
In the Red Sea, Iran-backed Yemen’s Houthi rebels haveopened a new front against Saudi Arabiaby declaring a naval blockade of the kingdom, threatening the flow of global oil supplies and trade. With the Strait of Hormuz blocked up, Saudi Arabia has been relying on a pipeline to the Red Sea to get millions of barrels of oil out to market.
A news agency in Yemen that is run by the Houthis, SABA, said Tuesday that six ships in the Red Sea were forced to reroute after they received warnings from the rebel group. The Associated Press could not immediately confirm the claim.
Three Houthi officials said the group warned international shipping companies on Monday to avoid transiting throughthe Bab el-Mandeb Strait, the gateway to the Red Sea on the opposite side of the Arabian Peninsula from the Strait of Hormuz. The officials spoke on condition of anonymity, saying they weren’t authorized to speak to journalists.
Momeni, Iran’s interior minister, metPakistan’s army chief Field Marshal Asim Munir, according to two officials who spoke on condition of anonymity to discuss the closed-door negotiations. Momeni later met Pakistan’s Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif.
In a statement, Sharif expressed deep concern over the recent U.S.-Iran escalation, and urged all sides to exercise restraint and avoid actions that could further destabilize the region.
Pakistan’s political leadership and Munir, who serves as both thechief of army staff and defense forces, played a key role in brokering last month’s interim agreement between the U.S. and Iran. Islamabad has been working to persuade both sides to return to the negotiating table.
Trump told reporters that Iranian officials “want to desperately meet. And until they’re ready to meet in a meaningful way, we have no interest in meeting.” He said the U.S. would soon target the area near Pickaxe Mountain — a fortified underground site near one of Iran’s primary nuclear enrichment facilities.
Iran’s military leaders said any attack on nuclear or “other sensitive facilities” would be considered a major escalation, according to a statement reported by Iranian state media.
A deep level of mistrust persists on both sides. Iran came under a surprise attack in June 2025 andagain in Februarywhile it was engaging in nuclear negotiations with the U.S. The interim deal unraveled when Iranresumed attacks on shipsin the Strait of Hormuz, which was supposed to be reopened under the agreement.
The British military’s United Kingdom Maritime Trade Operations center said a tanker came under attack early Tuesday in the strait off Oman, forcing the crew to abandon the vessel.
Iran’s paramilitary Revolutionary Guard claimed responsibility for the attack, as well as two other attacks on ships Monday in the waterway.
The route close to Oman’s shores has been the one the U.S. military has encouraged ships to travel to avoid Iran’s control.
To enforce the blockade in the strait, as of Tuesday U.S. forces have redirected eight commercial vessels and disabled one, CENTCOM said on X.
Ahmed reported from Islamabad. Contributing were Associated Press writers Fatma Khaled in Cairo; Sally Abou AlJoud in Beirut; Collin Binkley and Konstantin Toropin in Washington; and Christopher Weber in Los Angeles.

item.title: 希腊雅典卫城附近一名男子刺伤游客
item.description:
ATHENS, Greece (AP) — A man stabbed and wounded two tourists Tuesday morning near theancient Acropolisin Athens,Greece’stop tourist attraction, police said, in what appeared to be a random attack.
The assailant, identified only as a 60-year-old Greek man, attacked a Greek American couple with a knife on a pedestrian street near an entrance to the archaeological site and near the entrance to theAcropolis Museum,which lies at the foot of the Acropolis hill.
警方称，53岁男性受害者手臂受重伤，需要使用止血带，而48岁女性腿部受轻伤。两人均被救护车送往附近医院。据报道，两人的伤势都没有生命危险。
警方称，袭击者被当场拘留，并将于周二晚些时候出庭接受检察官正式指控。根据希腊关于识别嫌疑人的法律，当局没有提供有关他身份的进一步细节。目前尚不清楚袭击的动机，但警方表示，袭击者似乎患有心理问题。
美联社记者查尔斯·德莱德斯马报道称，一名男子在希腊顶级旅游景点雅典古卫城附近刺伤了两名游客。
该事件并未导致这些站点关闭，这些站点继续照常运行。希腊媒体报道称，袭击者据称随意用刀威胁路人，一名公众向警方报警。
袭击发生在当地时间上午 8 点刚过，当时古迹刚刚开放。清晨游览雅典卫城很受欢迎，因为这样可以避开夏季中午的炎热天气，因为山上几乎没有树荫。希腊目前正在经历夏季的第一波热浪，雅典的气温预计将达到 38 摄氏度（100 华氏度），该国部分地区的气温将达到 41 摄​​氏度（105 华氏度）。
希腊街头暴力袭击很少见。4 月，当局逮捕了一名 89 岁男子，据称他在雅典市中心的一家社会保障办公室和一家法院用猎枪开枪，造成至少 4 人受伤，据报道，他是因对社会保障不满而感到不满。
雅典卫城山上坐落着拥有近 2,500 年历史的帕特农神庙以及其他古迹，吸引了数百万游客。去年，大约有 460 万人访问了该网站。

item.title: 美国军方确认了第三名据信在伊朗袭击中丧生的军人身份
item.description:
北卡罗来纳州罗利（美联社）——美国军方已证实，据信有第三名军人在周五约旦基地袭击事件中死亡，并确认他们是中士。Angel S. Rampersad，28 岁，纽约人。
这一确认使自战争开始以来阵亡的美国军人人数达到 18 人。
周一，五角大楼确认了来自夏威夷埃瓦海滩的 25 岁第一中尉泰勒·詹姆斯·费汉 (Tyler James Feehan) 和列兵的身份。19 岁的伊莎贝拉·冈萨雷斯 (Isabella Gonzales) 来自德克萨斯州卡罗尔顿，她是周五袭击中在约旦丧生的另外两名士兵。The soldiers were killed byIranian ballistic missile and drone attacksin the first troop deaths from direct Iranian fire since the opening days of the war.美国中央司令部的一份声明称，他们被部署来支持美国打击伊斯兰国组织的任务。
袭击发生后，美国中央司令部宣布，两名军人死亡，另一人失踪。第二天，军事指挥部表示，部队发现了身份不明的遗骸，并正在对遗骸进行检查以核实身份。
现在，五角大楼表示，拉姆珀萨德被认为是失踪的士兵，她的官方身份现在是“职务状态 - 下落不明”，并且她“据信已经死亡”。
兰珀萨德与冈萨雷斯属于同一部队——位于德国安斯巴赫的第10陆军防空导弹防御司令部。
此外，一名在伊拉克因伊朗无人机受控爆炸而丧生的美国士兵被确认为中士。据周二的一份军方声明及其家人称，来自北卡罗来纳州的 30 岁父亲迈克尔·伊曼纽尔·斯文顿 (Michael Emmanuel Swinton) 已服役近十年。
斯文顿将获得铜星勋章、紫心勋章和战斗行动徽章，并将晋升为上士。陆军表示正在调查他周日在埃尔比勒空军基地死亡的情况。
他的母亲说，他留下了一个 6 岁的儿子和一个 4 岁的女儿。
“我永远无法从这件事中恢复过来，”他的妻子米娅·冈萨雷斯-斯文顿周一在脸书上发帖称。“你回来后我们有很多计划，我都恶心了。”她说她周日接到通知说他已被杀。
上周，两名美国陆军士兵也在约旦防御伊朗弹道导弹和无人机袭击时丧生。这是自战争爆发以来第一批美军因伊朗直接炮火而死亡。
他的母亲克里斯特尔·斯温顿·安杜哈尔 (Christel Swinton Andujar) 告诉美联社，他的妻子和孩子是他世界的中心。
她通过短信说：“他的遗产将通过他感动的无数生命、他分享的笑声、他给予他人的力量以及他对家人倾注的无条件的爱而继续存在。”“他将永远被怀念，永远受到尊敬，永远是我们的英雄。”
斯文顿于 2017 年入伍，被分配到北卡罗来纳州布拉格堡的第 55 防空炮兵团、第 108 防空炮兵旅。
根据公开记录，他住在斯普林湖，这是布拉格堡家庭在基地外很受欢迎的地方。军方将他的家乡称为费耶特维尔，这是附近的另一个社区。
“斯温顿中士是一位经验丰富的士官，他对自己的工作和周围的人感到非常自豪，”准将说。约翰·L·道伯将军，陆军防空与导弹防御司令部司令。“他以勇气、荣誉和无私奉献响应了职责号召。”
美国和伊朗之间的重燃战火已进入第十天，双方都发动了接二连三的打击。伊朗袭击了美国在该地区的盟友，美国士兵驻扎在中东各地。
伊朗当局表示，美国在过去三周内发动的袭击已造成至少 50 人死亡、500 多人受伤。
双方都把目标瞄准了数百万人依赖的民用基础设施。船上的工人以及海湾国家、以色列和黎巴嫩的外国工人和其他人也在冲突中丧生。
美国国务院在向美国人发出的新警告中表示，“伊朗和支持伊朗的团体可能会瞄准美国海外的其他利益或与美国和全世界美国人有联系的地点。”
美国总统唐纳德·特朗普表示，为了阻止伊朗发展核武器，这场战争是必要的。共和党人特朗普现在面临着结束战争并避免他竞选时反对的那种长期中东冲突的政治压力。最近几周，局势升级推高了油价。
这个故事已被更正以显示中士。迈克尔·伊曼纽尔·斯文顿周日去世，而不是军方早些时候报道的周六。
柯林斯从康涅狄格州哈特福德报道。

item.title: 被黄石野牛抛掷的男子称落地是最糟糕的部分
item.description:
看似平静地吃草的野牛却能够加速到可怕的速度，使它们成为黄石公园最危险的动物之一，最近有一头巨大的牛冲向并抛出了一位祖父。
65 岁的卡尔·麦克丹尼尔 (Carl McDaniel) 在 7 月 10 日国家公园的袭击中遭受多处骨折，但他活着讲述了这个故事，几天后他在蒙大拿州南部博兹曼健康女执事医疗中心的床上录制了一段视频。
“在它选择目标之前，他并没有表现出攻击性或类似的行为。那个目标恰好是我，”麦克丹尼尔周六在 Instagram 上发布的视频中说道。
袭击发生在距离黄石湖不远的桥湾露营地时，麦克丹尼尔正在和孙子一起散步。
“They move faster than you could ever imagine. He just kept coming,” he said.野牛没有与麦克丹尼尔的孙子联系，麦克丹尼尔没有受伤。
Bison 的奔跑速度可达 35 英里/小时（56 公里/小时），比男子 100 米短跑世界纪录还要快。它们站立时高达 6 英尺（2 米），体重达 2,000 磅（900 公斤），是北美最大的陆地动物。
黄石公园平均每年都会发生几次野牛袭击事件。2025 年，公园内至少有两人遭到袭击。
Bozeman Health emergency department Dr. Eric Lowe said in the video that McDaniel suffered fractures in multiple places.麦克丹尼尔说，最难的部分不是这种生物的角。
“他把我发射到空中并没有造成任何瘀伤，”他说。“着陆成为了问题。”

item.title: 特朗普宣布征收关税后，加拿大取消了与美国的联合开桥活动
item.description:
华盛顿（美联社）——在唐纳德·特朗普总统宣布对大多数加拿大商品征收 50% 的关税后，计划于周五举行的美加联合仪式庆祝连接底特律和安大略省温莎的戈迪·豪国际大桥开通，预计将不再举行。
在宣布关税时，特朗普指责加拿大不公平地歧视美国汽车、酒类和乳制品，加剧了与美国最亲密盟友之一的紧张关系。
加拿大基础设施部长格雷戈尔·罗伯逊 (Gregor Robertson) 发言人詹娜·加萨贝 (Jenna Ghassabeh) 在一份电子邮件声明中表示：“鉴于美国本周早些时候威胁采取贸易行动，两国之间继续举办庆祝活动是不合适的。”
在此之前，美国和加拿大官员本月早些时候就开放戈迪豪国际大桥达成协议，此前两国之间的分歧导致戈迪豪国际大桥的开通。
加拿大现在计划周五举行自己的仪式。目前尚不清楚美国是否会单独举行仪式。
两名不愿透露姓名的美国官员表示，他们仍预计大桥将于 7 月 27 日通车，但与之前相比，他们的确定性有所下降。
加萨贝表示：“我们仍然致力于在 7 月 27 日开通大桥，并在 7 月 24 日与加拿大人一起庆祝这一里程碑。”
这座长 1.5 英里（2.4 公里）的桥梁横跨底特律河，连接底特律和温莎。剪彩仪式原定于 6 月 12 日举行，但在官员表示美国和加拿大仍在努力解决“悬而未决的问题”后突然推迟。
加拿大根据一项协议资助了这座桥的建设，允许其通过通行费收入收回成本。特朗普政府曾寻求改变这一安排。
该项目由前密歇根州州长里克·斯奈德 (Rick Snyder) 谈判达成，自 2018 年起一直在建设中，耗资近 44 亿美元。
这座桥以加拿大冰球巨星戈迪·豪 (Gordie Howe) 的名字命名，他曾在底特律红翼队 (Detroit Red Wings) 度过了 25 个赛季。预计将成为加拿大与美国之间的又一条经济大动脉。
今年二月，特朗普在社交媒体上发帖要求加拿大给予美国政府至少一半的大桥所有权，并同意其他未具体说明的要求，这是他与加拿大在贸易问题上发生更广泛冲突的一部分。
这些紧张局势本周再次出现。白宫表示，关税将在 30 天内生效，为谈判留出时间。特朗普此前曾推迟或放弃了一些受到威胁的进口税。
加拿大总理马克·卡尼在一份关于关税的声明中表示，加拿大政府相信“自由和公平贸易的好处”，并仍准备与特朗普政府进行谈判。
随着加拿大考虑如何应对，关税可能升级为更广泛的贸易战。
安大略省省长道格·福特在社交媒体上发帖称：“如果这些关税继续下去，加拿大应该以一美元一美元的价格回应关税。”

item.title: 上诉法院驳回拜登阻止发布代笔采访录音的请求
item.description:
华盛顿（美联社）——联邦上诉法院驳回了乔·拜登提出的阻止公开他当选总统前回忆录代笔人采访录音和笔录的请求。
美国哥伦比亚特区巡回上诉法院由三名法官组成的分歧小组将其决定推迟到 8 月 3 日，以便让拜登有更多时间考虑再次上诉。
该小组周一晚间以 2 比 1 的裁决发现，披露拜登希望保密的材料符合“重大”公众利益。大多数人指出，对录音进行编辑将有助于保护拜登的隐私。
裁决称：“我们的结论是，因披露现已编辑的材料而对个人隐私造成的任何剩余侵犯可能不会超过披露的公众利益。”
美联社记者哈亚·潘瓦尼 (Haya Panjwani) 报道了乔·拜登在司法部调查中的上诉。
由民主党人拜登提名的法官弗洛伦斯·潘(Florence Pan)撰写了一份反对意见，她在其中表示，拜登在隐藏这些材料方面表现出了“巨大的隐私利益”。
她写道：“有争议的谈话发生在拜登的家中，政府在刑事调查过程中获得了录音，但并未导致起诉。”
华盛顿特区巡回法院首席法官 Sri Srinivasan 和法官 Gregory Katsas 也听取了上诉。斯里尼瓦桑由民主党总统巴拉克·奥巴马提名，并选择拜登作为副总统。卡萨斯是由共和党总统唐纳德·特朗普提名的，他在 2020 年总统选举中输给了拜登，但在 2024 年再次当选入主白宫。
马克·兹沃尼策 (Mark Zwonitzer) 曾与拜登合作撰写两本回忆录，分别是 2007 年的《要兑现的承诺》和 2017 年的《爸爸，答应我》。他于 2016 年和 2017 年在拜登家中采访了拜登。拜登的律师表示，这些谈话是坦诚的、私人的，并且旨在保密。
拜登发言人 T.J.达克洛表示，前总统不同意这一裁决，但“尊重法院以及独立司法机构在健康民主中发挥的重要作用。”
达克洛在一份声明中说：“十年前，拜登总统在一本书中讨论了他已故儿子的谈话，这些谈话是私人的，并已提供给司法部，但条件是保持这种状态。”“扭转方针并将其公开只是本届政府将（司法部）武器化以进行政治报复的最新例子。”
这些录音是由特别检察官罗伯特·胡尔获得的，他调查了拜登在担任特拉华州参议员和奥巴马副总统期间处理机密文件的情况。在胡尔拒绝对时任总统提出指控后，国会共和党人要求提供这些材料。
拜登提起诉讼，寻求禁令，阻止特朗普领导下的司法部向国会和保守派传统基金会公布这些录音。该部门此前辩称，根据公共记录法，这些录音可以免于披露。
特朗普提名的美国地区法官达布尼·弗里德里希 (Dabney Friedrich) 在 6 月份裁定，公众对这些材料的兴趣超过了拜登所拥有的任何隐私权，之后拜登提出上诉。
潘指出，多数人实际上是在裁定，拒绝拜登在上诉期间发出禁令的请求，支持立即披露这些材料。
“当然，这对这个案子来说是有争议的，”她写道。

item.title: 特朗普政府表示将推迟向加州和明尼苏达州支付 10 亿美元医疗补助
item.description:
The Trump administration on Tuesday said it was deferring more than $1 billion in Medicaid payments to Minnesota and California because of “suspected fraud and noncompliance,” the latest ina series of punitive stepsit has linked toallegations of fraudinmostly Democratic-led states.
Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. said the new actions — which come after previously announced Medicaid funding deferrals in those states — are part of the administration’s strategy to “stop the fraud before it happens” rather than claw back problematic spending after bad actors are prosecuted, as previous administrations had done.
“We have a duty to stop the payments, demand answers and then follow the evidence wherever it leads,” Kennedy told a news conference.
The tactic is part of the administration’s sweeping campaign to show it’s cracking down on fraud and saving taxpayers money when rising healthcare costs and other economic stressors have made affordability a top voter concern in November’s midterm elections.
In March, Vice President JD Vance launched a new anti-fraud task force at the request of President Donald Trump, bringing together officials from various departments to use data and technology to identify and investigate suspected wrongful use of federal dollars.
But the federal government hasn’t provided “data or explanation on how the deferral amount was calculated or what it was based on” to Minnesota, said John Connolly, the temporary commissioner and state Medicaid director for Minnesota’s Department of Human Services.
“Today’s actions show that the federal government is acting again in unprecedented and punitive ways as part of their war on Medicaid and its recipients,” he said in a statement. “Partnership — not politics — is required to stop criminals and protect services for the people who need them.”
Minnesota’s Democratic Gov. Tim Walz suggested the Republican administration was deferring the funds to pay for Trump’s tax cuts to wealthy Americans. California’s Democratic Gov. Gavin Newsom accused the administration of targeting it for political reasons.
The Trump administration intensified anti-immigration efforts in Minnesota earlier this year, sending in a robust force of Immigration and Customs Enforcement officers whose actions there caused weeks of counterprotests and included the shooting deaths of two civilians. Trump himself has frequently denounced California as a badly governed state.
The strategy has led to some false starts and headaches for states.
In April, for example, the Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services acknowledged to The Associated Press itmade a significant errorin figures it used to help justify a fraud probe in New York. Last month, California’s Medicaid director told a congressional committee that CMS had not yet provided “any instances of fraud, waste or abuse” to the state in its justification of a $1.3 billion Medicaid funding deferral announced in May.
And in Minnesota, state officials have been executing a thorough corrective action plan to defray CMS concerns that have led the agency to defer some $260 million in federal money and to threaten future cuts.
“We have cooperated in good faith and proactively engaged the Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services to first raise the alarm on fraud in Minnesota’s Medicaid program, to effectively investigate fraud and to institute further safeguards against future misuse of funds,” Connolly said.
Kennedy didn’t specify in Tuesday’s announcement whether the new deferrals — $867.5 million in federal Medicaid payments to California and $199 million to Minnesota — are in addition to, or overlap with,earlier deferralsannounced this year.
CMS Administrator Dr. Mehmet Oz didn’t provide concrete examples of fraud in the two states in justifying the deferrals. He did mention some patterns the agency noticed that it found questionable, including that some providers were billing for four or more patients at the same time, or billing after the date of a Medicaid beneficiary’s death.
Oz also highlighted a fast rate of growth in California’s home care program as a reason for concern. California officials have disputed that idea, explaining that the state’s home care program has grown because of an intentional strategy to keep people out of more expensive nursing homes.
“In-home care growth reflects intentional, federally encouraged expansion, not improper spending,” said Anthony Cava, spokesperson for the California Department of Health Care Services. “California is calling on CMS to stop threatening care for California’s most vulnerable residents.”
Kennedy and Oz said states could restart the flow of federal funding by providing documentation proving the payments in question were legitimate.
Officials in both states have previously acknowledged they are working with the federal government to provide the requested information. Oz said Minnesota had already returned documents to the federal government and they were being “reevaluated very carefully.”
Kennedy also suggested Tuesday that he would extend the power to exclude providers from Medicaid, Medicare and other federal health programs to CMS. In the past, that authority has rested solely with his department’s Office of the Inspector General.
“This is going to be a full force multiplier,” HHS Inspector General Thomas March Bell told a news conference. “It’s going to create additional momentum, and it’s going to exclude additional bad actors.”

item.title: 备忘录称 ESPN 因收购 NFL Network 而开始裁员
item.description:
ESPN 自 4 月份接管 NFL Network 以来，三年来首次进行大规模裁员。
美联社获得的董事长吉米·皮塔罗 (Jimmy Pitaro) 在周二上午给员工的一份备忘录中表示，大多数举措都是 ESPN 去年收购 NFL Network 和其他联盟数字资产的结果。
该交易于去年 8 月宣布，还包括 NFL Fantasy 以及向有线和卫星运营商分发 RedZone 频道的权利。作为回报，NFL获得了ESPN 10%的股权。
一月份它获得了政府监管机构的批准。NFL Network employees became part of ESPN on April 1.
皮塔罗在备忘录中写道：“在过去的几个月里，我们在将收购的 NFL 资产整合到 ESPN 方面取得了重大进展。”“在整个过程中，我们花时间仔细评估我们的集体团队、资源和组织结构，以便为我们的未来做好最佳定位。因此，我们不得不就我们今天将要传达的工作影响做出一些艰难的决定。
“虽然大部分工作影响与收购有关，但我们今天还将通知公司其他部门的同事，他们的职位受到了影响。我们致力于以同情心和尊重的态度对待员工，并在他们度过这一转变过程中提供支持。”
西雅图海鹰队主教练迈克·麦克唐纳（中）于 2026 年 2 月 26 日在印第安纳波利斯举行的 NFL Network 节目中发表讲话。（美联社照片/Julio Cortez，档案）
虽然大部分裁员影响的是制作和幕后人员，但一些播音人才也受到了影响。瑞安·克拉克 (Ryan Clark) 于 2015 年加入 ESPN，担任 NFL 分析师，他于周一在《NFL Live》节目中获悉自己被解雇的消息。A person familiar with the situation said Clark was originally going to be told Tuesday morning before reports leaked about the decision.
Clark was told by company executives rather than hear about it elsewhere.他没有完成在节目中的亮相。
该人士不愿透露姓名，因为他们无权讨论人事决定。
Clark took to social media on Tuesday to address the layoffs.他还分享了一段自己锻炼的视频。
“Sending prayers and love to all those laid off today by ESPN.你们中的许多人都将自己的生命倾注在这家公司中，我知道你们现在的感受，”克拉克在 X 上写道。“我的希望是，当这扇门关闭时，另一扇门会为你们所有人打开。愿上帝保佑！”
其他著名人物包括卡尔·拉维奇 (Karl Ravech)，他自 1993 年起就加入 ESPN，并成为其棒球报道中不可或缺的一部分。拉维奇是 2022-25 年《周日棒球之夜》的配音员，本赛季该节目转至 NBC 播出。He did callMonday night’s game between the Los Angeles Dodgers and Philadelphia Phillies.
自 2017 年以来一直在 NFL Network 工作的汤姆·佩利塞罗 (Tom Pelissero) 也在裁员之列。ESPN had recently signed NFL insiders Ian Rapoport and Adam Schefter to extensions.
The ESPN cutbacks are also part of other reductions that are taking place throughout the Walt Disney Company this week.

item.title: 世界杯决赛输给西班牙后，梅西返回阿根廷休息
item.description:
阿根廷罗萨里奥（美联社）——在世界杯决赛输给西班牙后，莱昂内尔·梅西于周二返回阿根廷休息几天。
梅西的私人飞机于早上6点27分从佛罗里达州劳德代尔堡降落在他的家乡罗萨里奥附近。尽管许多球迷举着标语和旗帜等待，但他并没有公开露面。
他在自己的 Instagram 账户上发帖称：“痛苦是巨大的，伤口需要时间才能愈合。”“我珍惜所有美好的时刻和将永远持续的记忆，以及整个国家的支持，再加上这个团队的辛勤工作和努力，推动我们再次跻身世界最佳之列。”
支持者们只瞥见了在停机坪上与梅西会面的SUV，将他和他的家人送往布宜诺斯艾利斯以北约300公里（185英里）的富内斯私人社区。
据报道，这位阿根廷队长与他的妻子安东内拉·罗库佐和三个孩子一起旅行，他们都陪伴他参加了整个北美比赛。在罗萨里奥等待他的是他 68 岁的父亲豪尔赫，他的家人在世界杯期间透露了他正在接受一种未公开的疾病的治疗。
凌晨3点30分，粉丝们就聚集在机场，希望一睹这位明星的风采。其中有两名身着Albiceleste球衣的年轻女孩举着欢迎牌，其中一张牌上写着：“梅西：你是一个传奇。感谢你给我们带来欢乐。整个国家都爱你！”
梅西预计将在七月底或八月初重新加入迈阿密国际队，参加正在进行的美国职业足球大联盟(MLS)赛季
阿根廷世界杯 26 名球员名单中的 15 名成员在主教练莱昂内尔·斯卡洛尼 (Lionel Scaloni) 的带领下于周一乘坐包机抵达布宜诺斯艾利斯埃塞萨机场。
周日，在新泽西州东卢瑟福举行的世界杯决赛中，西班牙队凭借费兰·托雷斯的进球以 1-0 输给了西班牙队。
斯卡罗尼于周二抵达他位于圣达菲省小镇普哈托的家，这是一件大事，他的邻居们聚集在一起欢迎他。
几分钟后，他走出家门，开始在人群递给他的阿根廷球衣上签名，并摆好姿势拍照。
“我们没有获胜，但我们可以感到满意。无法带来那种快乐是很痛苦的，”斯卡罗尼告诉记者。“我们输了，因为他们更好……也许我们已经筋疲力尽了。承认这一点很好——这会让我们变得更好。”
斯卡洛尼表示，他将履行与阿根廷足协 (AFA) 的合同，直至 12 月 31 日，但他没有确认是否会在该日期之后继续效力。“我会在这里待到十二月，伙计们……我们不要大惊小怪，”他补充道。
周日，梅西继 2014 年和 2022 年之后第三次闯入世界杯决赛。他以 8 个进球获得金靴亚军，落后法国队的基利安·姆巴佩 (Kylian Mbappé) 两个进球。
姆巴佩以 22 球高居世界杯历史进球榜榜首，比梅西多 1 球。
“现在很难完全欣赏我们的成就，但是这支球队连续两届进入世界杯决赛。我从心底感谢大家的每一条问候和信息，”梅西补充道。“我还要祝贺西班牙赢得冠军。”

item.title: 安全运动中心将开始公布其禁止名单上的人员姓名
item.description:
美国安全运动中心周二开始公布其纪律数据库中的人员名单，此举旨在提高透明度，并让人们更多地关注该中心认为是其打击性虐待的主要工具之一。
该中心负责打击奥林匹克运动中的虐待行为，长期以来一直发布所谓的集中纪律数据库。但在不公开名单上的人的情况下，人们必须访问数据库并知道他们在寻找谁，以了解他们周围被禁止和限制的人。
现在，该中心将每周分享有关添加到列表中的人员的最新信息；更新内容将包括已提交仲裁并已作出决定的案件。
该中心首席执行官贝尼塔·菲茨杰拉德·莫斯利 (Benita Fitzgerald Mosley) 表示：“知识就是力量，当信息尽可能容易获取时，维护安全的运动环境就会得到改善。”“通过分享已添加到集中纪律数据库的个人的每周新闻更新，我们希望通过提高公众意识来改善保护。”
收到该中心信件的包括国家管理机构、美国奥林匹克和残奥委员会以及媒体，这使得某人被列入数据库的消息更有可能被看到。
公开违规者是反兴奋剂的核心原则。公开宣布服用兴奋剂的人被认为是对吸毒的一种威慑，这种做法是世界禁毒规则的一部分。
安全体育中心在宣布这一变化的新闻稿中表示，数据库中列出的人员目前受到限制或禁止参加奥运会或残奥会运动——这一处罚涵盖了从精英到基层的各个阶层。该中心表示，该数据库是一个及时的快照，即名单上的某人可能会从其中消失，并且该中心的程序既不是刑事诉讼也不是民事诉讼。

item.title: 《哥斯拉》聋哑演员凯莉·霍特尔 (Kaylee Hottle) 18 岁时因车祸身亡
item.description:
弗雷德里克县治安官办公室称，因出演两部《哥斯拉》电影而闻名的聋哑演员凯莉·霍特尔周二凌晨在马里兰州的一场车祸中丧生。她18岁。
凌晨 3 点前，汽车冲出道路并撞上伊贾姆斯维尔的一个涵洞，霍特尔是车上两名乘客之一。据治安官办公室发布的新闻稿称，司机被送往当地一家医院，伤势没有生命危险，另一名乘客拒绝接受治疗。治安官办公室表示，据信超速是造成事故的一个因素，目前事故正在调查中。
霍特尔是美国手语的母语者，她的家人也是聋人。她的父母约书亚·霍特尔 (Joshua Hottle) 和凯西·霍特尔 (Ketsi Hottle) 在社交媒体网站上分享了有关她死亡的感人视频声明。
“凯莉的妈妈和四个兄弟姐妹都崩溃了，”她的父亲在机场发的一篇帖子中用美国手语说道。“他们都在家里，悲伤不已。我正在前往马里兰州的路上，将凯莉的遗体带回家。”
她的父亲说，霍特尔在从事故现场乘飞机抵达医院之前，她的心脏停止了跳动。“非常感谢我们一起度过的 18 年，并希望我们能拥有更多，”他说。
约书亚·霍特尔 (Joshua Hottle) 也联系了司机，治安官办公室称司机是一名 19 岁的弗雷德里克男子。
“我已经原谅你了，”她的父亲说。“别让这件事毁了你的余生。”
“我仍然觉得凯莉和我们在一起，”她的母亲在一篇帖子中用美国手语说道。
在《哥斯拉大战金刚》和续集《哥斯拉x金刚：新帝国》中，霍特尔扮演了一个名叫贾的孤儿女孩，她看穿了金刚威胁性的外表，并与怪物建立了特殊的联系，并用手语与它交流。
“听到这个消息我非常伤心。你会深深地想念凯莉，”她在《哥斯拉》中的搭档米莉·鲍比·布朗在 Instagram 上发帖说。
联合主演亚历山大·斯卡斯加德 (Alexander Skarsgård) 学习美国手语是为了与霍特尔交流，他在 2021 年告诉流行文化网站 Junkee，这位年轻天真的专业精神以及她快速将导演的笔记融入表演的能力给他和其他人留下了深刻的印象。
在 2024 年接受美联社采访时，霍特尔通过翻译表示，她在出演《哥斯拉》之前从未看过怪物电影，因此她的父亲帮助她了解了这一类型。
“我尊敬很多人，但我尊敬的一个人，我最尊敬的少数人，是我的父母和姐妹，”霍特尔说。
霍特尔说，当她拍摄第一部电影时，她有点担心之后是否能够恢复“正常生活”。
“人们确实在学校认出了我，是的，但这并不像我想象的那么糟糕，”霍特尔说。
霍特尔此前曾出演过 2021 年电视剧集《Magnum P.I.》她被列为出演即将上映的电影“什么不会杀死我们”。
“我非常心碎。在这个难以想象的时刻，我的心与她的家人同在，”她在这部电影中的联合主演、聋人演员阿拉夸·考克斯在 Instagram 上写道。
其他著名的聋人演员也在网上分享了悲伤的信息。
“我对可爱的凯莉·霍特尔的去世感到非常难过。愿她的美丽和才华成为永恒的记忆，”奥斯卡奖得主玛莉·马特林在 Instagram 上写道。
主演电影《尾声》的奥斯卡奖得主特洛伊·科瑟写道，霍特尔的去世让他想起了自己的女儿，并提醒他不要把时间视为理所当然。
科楚尔写道：“对于今天早上发生的事情，我无话可说。”他向霍特尔一家表示“最深切的哀悼”。
奥斯汀德克萨斯聋人学校的官员也在脸书上宣布了霍特尔的去世，并写道，她的同学和朋友将获得咨询和支持服务。
学校写道：“当我们共同哀悼这一巨大损失时，请惦念凯莉的亲人。”___ 布恩从爱达荷州博伊西报道。拉格从密歇根州安娜堡报道。

item.title: 侦探称 D4vd 在听证会上订购了链锯和裹尸袋，以决定歌手是否应受审
item.description:
LOS ANGELES (AP) —Singer D4vdordered a body bag, chain saws, an inflatable pool and a shovel online in the days after the death of 14-year-oldCeleste Rivas Hernandez, a homicide detective testified Tuesday.
Prosecutors say D4vd, a 21-year-old whose legal name is David Burke, used the items to dismember Rivas Hernandez’s body after he had stabbed her to death for threatening to reveal their relationship and ruin a music career that one witness said had brought in more than $10 million in just a couple of years.
On the first day of a hearing where a judge will decide if there’s enough evidence to go to trial, the homicide detective, Joshua Byers, testified that Burke had used the alias Trayvion Davis for the orders, but used his own cards to buy them and had them delivered to his Hollywood home.
Burke’s lawyers have said hedid not cause Rivas Hernandez’s death, and he has pleaded not guilty to murder, continuous sexual abuse of a child under the age of 14 and unlawful mutilation of human remains.
Byers said most of the items that digital records show Burke bought, including two of the three chain saws and the blue pool, had been found in Burke’s garage when a warrant was served. Prosecutors previously said a blood stain in the garage matched Rivas Hernandez’s DNA.
And Byers testified that the body bag Burke ordered matched one that contained the head and torso of Rivas Hernandez in the trunk of a Tesla registered to Burke at aHollywood tow yardon Sept. 8, 2025.
The lead prosecutor, Deputy District Attorney Beth Silverman, showed photos in court of the dismembered and decomposing body of Rivas Hernandez. Under the body bag, authorities found agarbage bagwith the girl’s arms and legs.
Burke’s lawyer, Blair Berk, pressed Byers about whether the body bag was tested to tie it to the defendant. Berk’s cross-examination took a microscopic look at how the scene was treated and the body was investigated.
Byers said the medical examiner at the scene told him the bag could not be taken into evidence because the body was so decomposed it could not be removed from it.
Rivas Hernandez’s parents were in court along with dozens of reporters and members of the public who got their seats through a lottery drawing. Her mother appeared to look down when the photos of the body were displayed.
Investigators did not immediately identify the body of Rivas Hernandez when it was found. Byers said her father, Jesus Rivas, called police a few days after the body’s discovery because he had seen news reports and feared it was his daughter.
The photos showed blue fragments on the body that prosecutors say were from the inflatable pool.
Byers also testified that when he and his partner arrested Burke, they noticed he had ‘Celeste’ tattooed in red on his finger. A photo of it was shown in court. A tattoo on another finger read “Shhh” and was identical to one on Rivas Hernandez’s finger.
Also Tuesday, prosecutors showed a body camera video from February 2024 with Burke telling sheriff’s deputies who were investigating after the then-13-year-old Rivas Hernandez was reported missing; Burke told them he had only met the girl once in person, and did not know she was a minor.
“She told me she was 18,” the singer said in the video from February 2024. “Everybody’s telling me she was 13.”
Prosecutors allege that D4vd had already been having a sexual relationship with the girl for several months. He was 18 at the time. Burke said he met the girl as a fan online on Discord and Instagram in 2020 or 2021.
LA County Sheriff’s Deputy Juan Gonzalez, whose body camera video was shown, testified that Burke had been extremely cooperative, and allowed him and his partner to search the house. They found no sign of Rivas Hernandez, who was reported missing a number of times in the years before her death.
The mood was friendly and jovial among the three, and one of the deputies says “Ohhh, that’s pretty cool, good for you,” when Burke described his music career.
The preliminary hearing, which is expected to last about four days, resembles an abbreviated trial.Judge Charlaine Olmedowill decide whether the prosecution offered enough evidence to demonstrate probable cause to proceed to trial.
District Attorney Nathan Hochman said outside the hearing that his office has not yet decided whether to pursue the death penalty in the case.
The defendant, who has been held without bail in a jail near the courthousesince his arrest, appeared in court in his Orange jail clothes and glasses. The judge ruled that his hands could be uncuffed, but he must remain shackled to his chair.
A court filing said Burke sent a car to pick up Rivas Hernandez on April 23, 2025, from her hometown of Lake Elsinore, California, some 80 miles (129 km) outside Los Angeles. They exchanged messages until she arrived at his home, after which her phone went dead.
“Knowing he had to silence the victim before she ruined his music career as she had threatened,” the document said, “defendant stabbed the victim to death multiple times and stood by while she bled out.”
One of Burke’s financial managers, Benjamin Gregor, testified that he had earned between $10 million and $11.5 million dollars from 2023 through 2025.
D4vdbegan making music for YouTube videos he created of the video game Fortnite.
The songs he wrote and recorded on his phone were a blend of indie rock, R&B and lo-fi pop. The music made him a phenomenon on TikTok, Soundcloud and Spotify, where his top songs, including his 2022 breakthrough “Romantic Homicide,” have more than a billion plays.
In 2023, he released two EPs and opened for SZA on tour. Last year, he performed at the Coachella music festival and released a full album that he was on tour promoting when the body was discovered.

item.title: 电影评论：匹克球是体育喜剧《丁克人》的明星
item.description:
《丁克人》是《躲避球：一个真正的失败者故事》的精神后裔，讲述的是网球团体在一个紧张的俱乐部里对抗匹克球爱好者的故事。这个故事本身非常符合书本：一位曾经前途无量的网球神童学会了克服自己和他对“次要运动”的蔑视。
在世界的一个版本中，它可能是“前沿”。但《丁克人》选择了接地气、傻乎乎的路线，有着令人难忘的古怪角色和一些高调的客串，这些都构成了一部令人愉快的观看。
杰克·约翰逊饰演达斯蒂，他专横的父亲查克（艾德·哈里斯饰）从他出生起就一直在培养他成为网球明星。在一段有趣的介绍中，我们得知，早在他告诉儿子自己的真名之前，他就称呼他的儿子为“锤子”。但与许多体育神童一样，达斯蒂很早就熄火了（在一场与年轻的安迪·罗迪克的比赛中，同样如此）。现在，他在父亲的俱乐部里给孩子们教网球课，并给他们讲述他辉煌岁月的故事，让他们感到无聊。他只剩下一个朋友：PJ，一位由澳大利亚喜剧演员陈亚伦饰演的热心阿谀奉承、心地善良的怪人。
达斯蒂和他的父亲非常讨厌匹克球，所以当达斯蒂的医生（由本·斯蒂勒担任制作人的简短而有趣的出场）建议将其作为手腕受伤的康复治疗时，这是一个比死亡更糟糕的判决。当然，当他开始与一群年长的女性一起演奏时，包括坎迪斯（玛丽·斯汀伯根饰）、盖尔（克莱奥·金饰）和朱迪（已故的琳恩·玛丽·斯图尔特饰），他最终会改变自己的态度。
约翰逊非常擅长扮演并不总是讨人喜欢的失败者。他的一位新朋友说她认为他看起来像蒂莫西·查拉梅 (Timothée Chalamet)；他笑道，以前从来没有人这么说过或这么想过。但她可能说得对：达斯蒂属于马蒂至尊谱系。
斯汀伯根也可爱又有趣，而哈里斯则是完美的老爸；难道就不能有更多的电影人和编剧想办法好好利用我们七十多岁的老人吗？
这部电影中也有很多喜剧演员扮演小角色：帕顿·奥斯瓦尔特是俱乐部的常驻泡菜球爱好者，克洛伊·法恩曼是一个疯狂的前女友，克里斯·帕内尔是一名网球职业选手。该片由乔什·格林鲍姆执导，肖恩·克莱门茨编剧，在愚蠢与真诚之间取得了一定的成功。而且，值得赞扬的是，它并没有低到让匹克球及其球员成为每个笑话的笑柄。当然，有一些；但大部分都是做得如此有爱。
在这一切之中，有一个序列可能是今年最有趣的部分。我不会残忍到破坏我正在谈论的内容（或者大胆地认为言语可能会公正地表达），但我会说它不涉及任何主要角色，并且与故事没有什么关系。这真是荒谬又完美。
格林鲍姆也是《倒钩和明星去维斯塔德尔玛》和即将上映的《太空球》续集的幕后制作人，他正在为自己作为可靠的喜剧导演开辟一个良好的空间，这种类型在大银幕上几乎濒临灭绝。
对于电影来说，这是一个奇怪的时刻：人们对院线电影的兴趣显然已经存在，而不仅仅是《奥德赛》。然而，本周有两部主流喜剧，包括凯文·哈特的单身派对电影《72 小时》，其明星阵容将直接进入流媒体。一线希望是，人们仍在制作直接喜剧，但当他们完全跳过电影院时，就会失去一些东西。当然，您可以将家人聚集在客厅，但并非所有家庭都具有相同类型的幽默。如果你周围的人不开心，你就很难屈服于某件事。在剧院里，至少有机会与其他志趣相投的人一起欢笑，甚至呻吟。（我无法评价《72小时》的质量。）
有些电影仍然有机会，比如《盖尔·道特里和名人性爱通行证》，但当大而愚蠢的喜剧不再是常规节目的一部分时，很难相信看电影会真正“回归”。
周五在 Apple TV 上播出的《丁克》因“粗俗语言和色情内容”被美国电影协会评为 PG-13。片长：102分钟。两星半（满分四星）。

item.title: ChatGPT 制造商称 OpenAI 模型自行侵入另一家人工智能公司
item.description:
ChatGPT 制造商 OpenAI 周二表示，其人工智能系统自行入侵了另一家人工智能公司，该公司称之为“前所未有的网络事件”。
OpenAI 首席执行官萨姆·奥尔特曼 (Sam Altman) 在社交媒体上发布的一份声明中表示：“我们在评估模型期间发生了重大安全事件。”
人工智能初创公司 Hugging Face 上周表示，它检测到对其数据处理系统的入侵，怀疑是由人工智能代理自主行动造成的。
Hugging Face 联合创始人兼首席执行官克莱门特·德兰格 (Clément Delangue) 在一份声明中表示：“考虑到该特工的复杂程度，我们怀疑上周的网络攻击可能来自前沿实验室。”“原来是这样！”
这一披露正值人们对强大模型的网络安全能力的高度担忧之际，唐纳德·特朗普总统于 6 月签署了一项行政命令，为联邦政府创建一个框架，在最先进的人工智能系统公开发布前长达一个月的时间内审查其国家安全风险。
OpenAI 在周二的声明中表示：“人工智能正在加速漏洞的发现和利用。”“这次事件的主要教训是，模型安全必须跟上快速发展的能力。”
Delangue 表示，过去 24 小时他一直在与 OpenAI 合作，“我们坚信他们没有恶意。这一切都是自主发生的，真是令人兴奋！”
德兰格补充说，这“可能是此类事件的第一例”。
OpenAI 表示，此次入侵是由其人工智能模型组合造成的，包括新发布的 GPT-5.6 Sol 和仍在内部测试的“功能更强大”的模型。
OpenAI 表示，其人工智能使用被盗的凭据，并发现了一个先前未知的漏洞来访问 Hugging Face 服务器。
该公司表示，它“不遗余力地实现了相当狭窄的测试目标”，并“找到了获取秘密信息的方法，并利用这些信息来欺骗评估”。
